segments.s548-g49: همه یاد دارید گفتار ما is a topical clause: همه یاد دارید = 'you all remember' (یاد = 'memory/remembrance' plus دارید = present 'have' used idiomatically 'to remember'), followed by گفتار ما = 'our speech/words' (گفتار = noun, ما is the possessive). Word order places the verb phrase یاد دارید before the noun phrase گفتار ما for emphasis.
segments.s548-g50: کشیدن بدین کار تیمار ما is an infinitival (or verbal-noun) clause that functions as the content of 'our words' (i.e. 'our saying is: ...'). کشیدن is the verbal noun/infinitive 'to take up / to proceed with' (classical Persian uses infinitives as complements); بدین = به + این ('to/with this') is a classical locative/ directional phrase, کار = 'task/work', and تیمار ما = 'our care/concern' where the copula is omitted (typical in Persian) so the infinitive clause stands as predicate: 'to take up this work is our concern.'
segments.s548-g51: هرآن کس که ... is a relative clause 'whoever' (هرآن کس که = 'whosoever'). با داد و روشن دلید modifies that subject: با introduces accompanying qualities 'with justice and a clear heart', where داد = 'justice' and روشن دلید = 'with a clear/bright heart delights' (دلیدن = 'to take delight / to be pleased' used here intransitively).
segments.s548-g52: از آمیزش یکدگر مگسلید is an admonition addressed to the addressees. از آمیزش یکدگر = 'from the union/coming together of one another' (آمیزش = 'union/coming together', یکدگر = 'one another'). مگسلید is a negative-present form meaning 'do not break / do not separate' (classical Persian uses the prefix م- to negate an imperative/subjunctive); here it functions as a prohibitive: 'do not sever.'
segments.s548-g53: دل آرام دارید بر چار چیز is literally 'have/keep your heart calm upon four things.' دل آرام = 'a calm heart' (دل = heart, آرام = calm), دارید = present 'you have/keep' (second-person plural/polite), بر = preposition 'about/regarding', چار چیز = 'four things' (چار here enumerates 'four'). The verb دارید governs the prepositional phrase بر چار چیز.
segments.s548-g54: کزو خوبی و سودمندیست نیز is a relative/explanatory clause 'from which there is goodness and also benefit.' کزو = که + زو = 'from which/of which', خوبی = 'goodness' (or 'good'), و = 'and', سودمندیست = predicate 'is beneficial / brings advantage' (sودمندی + ست), and نیز = 'also/too' adds emphasis that both goodness and usefulness come from those things.
segments.s548-g55: یکی بیم و آزرم و شرم خدای enumerates the first of the four things: یکی = 'one (item)/first', followed by a list 'بیم و آزرم و شرم' = 'fear, [reverent] awe, and modesty/shame', and خدای qualifies them 'of God / toward God.' Note: آزرم is a rare/poetic term here meaning reverence/awe and is not a common modern lemma.
segments.s548-g56: که باشد ترا یاور و رهنمای is a relative clause describing the effect: که = 'which/that', باشد = third-person optative/subjunctive 'may be / will be' (classical flavor; functions like 'is/will be'), ترا = archaic object-form 'to you' (تو + را), یاور و رهنمای = 'helper and guide'. Word order places the predicate (باشد ...) after the relative marker for emphasis.
segments.s548-g1: به is the preposition 'to/toward' taking the following noun phrase; گفتار is 'speech/words'. این modifies نامدار (this renowned), which in turn qualifies the proper name اردشیر. The whole phrase = 'to the words of this renowned Ardashir.'
segments.s548-g2: همه 'everyone/all' is the addressed group; گوش دارید literally 'you have ears' (دارید present-tense 2pl) = 'you listen/pay attention.' برنا و پیر ('young and old') enumerates the addressees. The verb is present indicative but functions as an exhortation to the audience.
segments.s548-g3: هرانکس 'whoever' with a relative/complementizer که introduces the clause. داند is the present 3rd-person verb 'knows'; the second که introduces the content clause 'that دادار هست' (that the Creator exists), where هست is the copula 'is/exists.'
segments.s548-g4: نباشد is a negative-form verb ('be not' — optative/jussive sense here: 'should not be' / 'ought to be nothing but'); مگر means 'except' and introduces the required predicates پاک و یزدان‌پرست ('pure and God‑worshipper/pious'). The clause prescribes the only acceptable state for such a person.
segments.s548-g5: دگر 'another' / 'further' introduces an additional item to know. آنک is a relative 'that/who'; دانش مگیرید uses the imperfective prefix مـ plus the verb 'گرفتن' ('take/receive') to mean 'acquires/accepts knowledge' (magi-rid is present/imperfect form). خوار is the predicate 'despised/lowly.'
segments.s548-g6: اگر introduces a conditional 'if/when' and زیردستست is a fused phrase 'is subordinate/underling' (زیردست + است). و‌گر ('or if') contrasts with شهریار 'a king/ruler', so the clause says the contempt applies whether the person is lowly or a ruler.
segments.s548-g7: سه دیگر 'three more' (things) plus بدانی (subjunctive/optative 'you should know' or hortative 'know') introduces the list. که begins the subordinate clause; هرگز 'never' modifies سخن 'speech/talk.' The phrase functions as 'Know three other things: that never (will) speech...'.
segments.s548-g8: نگردد is the negative-present/subjunctive form of گشتن 'to turn/become' (here 'will not be turned'); بر is the preposition 'upon/against' and مرد دانا کهن is the noun phrase 'a man, wise and old' (adjectives follow the noun). The clause means 'speech will never be turned against an old wise man.'
segments.s548-g73: This is the conditional front (introduced by گر = "if") followed by sources: از کارداران وز لشکرش = "from his ministers and from his army." In classical Persian وز here functions like و از (and from); word order places the prepositional phrases before the verb which comes later.
segments.s548-g74: بداند is a 3rd-person present (classical form) meaning "he knows/learns." که introduces the content clause. رنجست is a predicative nominal construction (رنج + -ست = "there is suffering"), followed by the preposition بر + کشور + ـش = "upon his country/realm."
segments.s548-g75: نیازد is a 3rd-person present of نیازیدن here meaning "seeks/asks for" (i.e., takes action to obtain). The phrase به داد او = "for his justice / on his behalf." جهاندار نیست is the predication: "he is not a world-ruler/sovereign." The whole clause states that failing to seek justice for a subject disqualifies one as a ruler.
segments.s548-g76: This is the consequence clause: تاج شاهی سزاوار نیست = "he is not worthy/deserving of the kingly crown." ورا (in the previous segment) supplies the dative/beneficiary sense for this clause as well. Note: برو in the manuscript here is an archaic/clitic item whose sense does not match the simple verb senses given; the core meaning is that he is not deserving of the crown.
segments.s548-g77: سیه کرد is a verbal phrase meaning literally "made (it) black" and is used figuratively here as "sullied/tainted." منشور شاهنشهی is a genitive construction = "the imperial charter/edict" (منشور modified by شاهنشهی). Word order is verb + object.
segments.s548-g78: ازان پس = "from that time on / thereafter." نباشد is a negative existential/continuative meaning "there is no longer / does not remain." ورا is the dative pronoun "for him," and فرهی here denotes honour/nobility. Together: "thenceforth no honour remained for him."
segments.s548-g79: چنان دان is an imperative/hortative construction meaning "consider/think him thus." که introduces the illustrative clause describing how to regard him. بیدادگر شهریار = "the unjust/tyrannical sovereign" functions as the subject of the following clause.
segments.s548-g80: بود is the copula (here past/basic copular sense: "was/was like"). شیر درنده is the noun + adjective "a ferocious lion," and در مرغزار is a locative prepositional phrase "in the meadow." Note the poetic inversion (verb before subject) common in classical Persian.
segments.s548-g25: کزین بگذری is a conditional/temporal phrase: کز (from/of) + این (this) + بگذری (you pass) — here بگذری is a present/subjunctive 2nd-person form used poetically for ‘if/when you pass’. پنج رایست پیش is a verbless/predicative phrase where رای است is fused as رایست (archaic/poetic), with پنج (five) before the noun (numeral + noun order) and پیش marking ‘ahead/before’ — altogether: ‘beyond this five counsels lie ahead.’
segments.s548-g26: کجا is the interrogative ‘where’. تازه گردد is a present/optative/impersonal form of گردیدن/گشتن meaning ‘become/made new’ (here used as future/result). ترا is split into the enclitic ت (second-person dative/benefactive suffix attached to the verb) plus را (object/topic marker) and marks ‘for you’; دین و کیش are the coordinated objects ‘religion and ritual/creed’. Word order places the interrogative first, verb near it, and the object marked by را.
segments.s548-g27: تن آسانی و شادی places the affected nouns (تن ‘body’, آسانی ‘ease’, شادی ‘joy’) before the verb, a normal Persian order for emphasis. افزایدت is افزاید (3rd‑person singular present/future ‘adds/increases’) with the enclitic ت ‘to/for you’, so the clause means ‘(he/it) will add ease and joy to your body.’
segments.s548-g28: که introduces a clause of explanation or consequence (‘for/that’). با شهد او is an instrumental phrase ‘with his honey/sweetness’ (با + object + possessive او). زهر is the subject ‘poison’, and نگزایدت is negative + گزاید (3rd‑person, ‘harm/afflict’) plus the enclitic ت ‘you’ — literally ‘poison will not harm you’. The negative prefix ن is fused to the verb in old/poetic morphology.
segments.s548-g29: یکی is an enumerator ‘one’ (one of the counsels). آنک is a relative/demonstrative ‘that which/that who’ (archaic form). از بخشش دادگر is a prepositional phrase ‘from/by the generosity/bounty of the just (one)’ — بخشش is ‘grant/donation’ and دادگر functions as an epithet ‘the just (ruler)’. Together: ‘One [is that which] by the just one’s bounty…’.
segments.s548-g30: به آز و به کوشش uses repeated به as an instrumental/benefactive marker ‘by/with greed and with effort’ (two parallel prepositional phrases). نیابی is ن + یابی (you do/you find) negated, here 2nd‑person present/subjunctive/indicative ‘you will not find/obtain’. گذر is the noun ‘passage/way/success’ and is the object of نیابی: ‘you will not find a way by greed or toil’ — i.e., you cannot succeed through greed or mere effort when bounty is given.
segments.s548-g31: This is a compact two-part clause: توانگر شود (literally "becomes wealthy") is the main verb phrase expressing a general truth, followed by a relative/indefinite subject introduced after it. هرک is an archaic contracted form of هر که "whoever," and خرسند گشت is a clause meaning "became content/was content." Note that شود is the present/subjunctive/generalizing use of شدن and گشت is the perfective/past form of گشتن used here idiomatically to state a general result — together the line reads as a proverb: "whoever is/content became content, (thereupon) becomes wealthy."
segments.s548-g32: گل نوبهارش is a genitive/possessive grouping: گل "flower" + نوبهارش (نوبهار + enclitic ـش) = "the flower of his/its new spring." برومند is an adjectival/predicative meaning "prosperous/ flourishing," and گشت (past of گشتن) completes the predicate "became/grow(ed) prosperous." The clause parallels the first, using poetic inversion and resultative past to state a general observation.
segments.s548-g41: همه is a vocative/subject 'all/everyone'. گوش دارید is literally 'have ears' (گوش + دارید) — a common Persian idiom meaning 'pay attention' or 'listen'; داريد is 2nd-person plural present of داشتن 'to have'. پند مرا places the noun 'پند' (advice) before 'مرا' (the poetic/old form of the possessive/object element), so 'پندِ من' (my advice).
segments.s548-g42: سخن گفتن is a nominalized verb phrase 'the speaking / saying of words' (سخن = speech, گفتن = to say). سودمند is an adjective 'useful/beneficial' modifying that verbal noun. مرا again links the speech to the speaker ('of me' / 'my'); the copula is omitted (typical in Persian poetry), so the phrase reads as '(the) speaking of useful words of mine (is) beneficial.'
segments.s548-g43: بود is the copula 'was/is'. بر دل هرکسی is a locative expression 'on/in every heart' (بر + دل + هرکسی), meaning 'to everyone' or 'in every heart'. ارجمند is a predicate/adjective 'esteemed/precious' describing what 'بود' refers to (something was held dear).
segments.s548-g44: که introduces a subordinate clause 'that/so that'. یابند is 3rd-person-plural present-subjunctive/optative of یافتن 'to find/obtain' (here 'may find/obtain'). ازو is the old/poetic fused form of از + او 'from him'. ایمنی از گزند = 'safety from harm' (ایمنی 'safety', از 'from', گزند 'harm').
segments.s548-g45: زمانی here functions adverbially 'for a while / for some time'. میاسای is an imperative/exhortation 'be at rest / cease' (archaic/poetic form). ز آموختن uses the poetic preposition ز = 'from' before the verbal noun آموختن 'learning', so the phrase means 'rest from learning'.
segments.s548-g46: اگر is the conditional 'if'. جان is 'life' or 'soul' (object of wish). همی خواهی is the archaic-present 2nd-person singular 'you desire/wish' (همی is an older present marker + خواهی 'you want'). افروختن is an infinitive 'to kindle/ignite' used here figuratively 'to enliven/awaken'. Together: 'if you wish to enliven the soul'.
segments.s548-g47: چو is a subordinating particle 'when/if'. فرزند is 'child/offspring'. باشد is the (subjunctive/present) form of 'to be' here introducing the circumstantial clause 'when the child is...'. به فرهنگ دار combines به 'in/under' + فرهنگ 'culture/education/upbringing'; دار here is part of a verbal/adjectival predicate meaning 'keep/retain' (poetic usage) — overall: 'when a child is in (your) upbringing/care.'
segments.s548-g48: زمانه here refers to 'the world/one's fortunes' (usage: worldly affairs). ز بازی = 'from play' (ز = from, بازی = play/frivolity). برو تنگ دار is an imperative sequence: برو (go away / remove) and تنگ دار 'keep tight/close' (i.e. keep him constrained/not given to play). In sum: protect the child's worldly standing by keeping him away from play and holding him tightly/strictly.
segments.s548-g81: همان functions as a demonstrative ('that/the same') modifying the noun زیردستی ('underling'). که introduces a relative clause modifying زیردستی. فرمان شاه is a noun phrase 'the king's command' (فرمان governed by شاه in an attributive/genitive relation).
segments.s548-g82: به ... و به ... is an adverbial phrase meaning 'with / by means of ... and ...' (here 'with hardship and with effort'). ندارد is the present negative of داشتن ('does not have') and here combines with the noun نگاه to mean 'pays no heed / has no regard'; نگاه is the noun 'look/regard' used idiomatically.
segments.s548-g83: بود is the copular verb 'to be' here used in an optative/wishful context ('may it be' — a common poetic use). زندگانیش is a possessive formation meaning 'his living / his life' (زندگان + ـش). با درد و رنج is a prepositional phrase 'with pain and suffering' modifying زندگانیش.
segments.s548-g84: نگردد is a negative jussive/subjunctive form of گشتن here meaning 'may he not become / may he not grow (old)'. کهن is the predicate adjective 'old/aged'. در سرای سپنج is a locative phrase 'in the house (saray) of sponge' — a poetic compound; سرای + سپنج names the place.
segments.s548-g85: اگر marks a conditional 'if'. مهتری is a noun meaning 'a superior / elder / chief'. یابد is the present/subjunctive form of یافتن 'he finds' (here within the protasis). و connects the two coordinated ideas; بهتر is the comparative adjective 'better'; the final ی is an archaic/expletive copular/existential element ('there is / is') completing the elliptical clause.
segments.s548-g86: نیابد is the negative form of یابد ('does not find'). به before the personal names here functions as a comparative/reference marker ('than / compared to') pointing to Zefti and Kandauri; the two names are coordinated by و. The clause as a whole says he does not find anyone better than those two.
segments.s548-g87: دل زیردستان ما is a genitive construction 'the hearts of our subordinates' (دل + زیردستان + possessive ما). شاد باد is an optative formula 'may (they) be happy' where باد is the wish/optative particle following the adjective.
segments.s548-g88: هم 'also/likewise' links the second wish to the first. از marks the cause or instrument here ('by/through/from' our justice). داد ماگیتی is a compact phrase meaning 'our justice' (داد) with گیتی 'the world' in the surrounding sense; آباد باد is again optative 'may (it) be prosperous' — overall: 'and may the world be prosperous through our justice.'
segments.s548-g33: دگر here functions as a temporal/continuative adverb ('then/after that' or 'again') introducing what follows. بشکنی is a classical second-person singular verb form (subjunctive/temporal) from شکستن; in this construction it carries the sense 'when/after you have broken' rather than a simple present. گردن آز را is a direct object phrase: گردن 'neck' + آز 'greed', with the object marker را marking the whole phrase as what is broken.
segments.s548-g34: نگویی is a negative present/subjunctive/imperative form (the classical negative with ن-), meaning 'do not say / do not tell'. به پیش زنان is a prepositional phrase 'before/in the presence of women' (به + پیش = 'in front of'), and راز را is the definite object 'the secret' marked by را. Word order is verb (negated) + prepositional phrase + object (poetic order).
segments.s548-g35: دیگر here functions as an enumerative/continuative element ('another/third in the series' in the poem). ننازی is a negative imperative/subjunctive (ن- + نازی) meaning 'do not show pride / do not boast / do not be coy'. به ننگ و نبرد is the prepositional object 'in (or about) shame and battle'.
segments.s548-g36: که introduces an explanatory/causal clause 'for/since'. ننگ ونبرد repeats the paired nouns 'shame and warfare' (ونبرد = 'and battle/war' here). آورد is the simple present/present-habitual 'brings/causes'. رنج و درد are the coordinated results 'suffering and pain'.
segments.s548-g37: که here introduces the content of the fourth precept. دل دور داری ز غم is a verbal clause: دل 'heart' (subject/theme) + دور داری (دور + داری) 'you keep distant' with داری as the present second-person carrying 'keep/have', and ز غم 'from sorrow' is the ablative/complement introduced by poetic ز.
segments.s548-g38: ز نا آمده = 'from that which has not come' or 'about the not-yet-arrived'; نا + آمده = 'not come/yet to come'. دل نداری is 'do not have a heart' (i.e. 'do not let your heart be'), where نداری expresses lack/negation. دژم is a predicate adjective 'sullen, grim' attached to دل. The clause thus forbids being sullen over things that haven't occurred.
segments.s548-g39: نه is the prohibitive particle 'do not'. پیچی (from پیچیدن) here means roughly 'turn yourself toward / meddle' and is used in the negative imperative sense 'do not involve yourself'. به کاری is 'in/into a matter', and که کار تو نیست is a relative clause 'which is not your affair' (که introducing 'which').
segments.s548-g40: نتازی is another prohibitive form (ن- + تازى) meaning 'do not rush/attack'. بدان is the fusion of ب + دان (classical to/that) i.e. 'upon that'. کو functions as a relative/where/which here ('that which'), and شکار تو نیست completes the thought 'is not your prey/quarry'. The whole clause: 'do not rush at something that is not your prey.'
segments.s548-g65: ورا is a prepositional/pronominal particle meaning 'to him/it' placed before the comparative clause. چون functions here as a comparative 'as/like', تن خویش is 'your own body' (خوی + enclitic ـش = 'own'), and خواهی is 2nd-person present 'you wish/want'; به مهر is an adverbial phrase 'with love/affection'. Together this clause gives a conditional/comparative protasis: 'If you desire/love him as your own body...'.
segments.s548-g66: به فرمان او is a prepositional phrase 'by/at his command' (به + فرمان + enclitic او). تازه گردد uses گردیدن in a classical optative/3rd-person form meaning 'becomes / will become / is renewed' (verb at the end); سپهر is the subject 'the sky/realm/heavens'. The clause is the apodosis: 'at his command the realm is renewed.'
segments.s548-g67: دلت is دل + 2nd-person enclitic ـت 'your heart'. بسته داری is adjective + verb 'keep ... bound' (بسته 'bound/tied' + داری 'you have/keep'), and به پیمان اوی is 'to his covenant' with اوی an older possessive form. The clause is an imperative/advisory: 'keep your heart bound to his pledge.'
segments.s548-g68: روان را uses را as the definite/object marker ('your spirit/soul' as the object). نپیچی is the negative present/jussive form of پیچیدن (with prefix ن-) meaning 'do not turn/swerve'. ز فرمان اوی is a prepositional phrase 'from his command'. The whole clause: 'do not let your soul turn away from his command.'
segments.s548-g69: برو here is a discourse particle (often 'then' / 'so' / 'go on') introducing the admonition. مهر داری is 'you have love' or 'cherish', withداری as the present-tense auxiliary. چو introduces the comparison 'as', and بر جان خویش literally 'upon/for your life' = 'as you do for your own life' (خوی + enclitic ـش = 'your own'). The clause advises: 'So love him as you love your life.'
segments.s548-g70: چو here means 'when/as'. با داد is an adverbial 'with justice' (با + داد). بینی is 2nd-person present of دیدن 'you see'. نگهبان خویش is 'your guardian/protector' (نگهبان + خویش). The clause is conditional/temporal: 'when you see your guardian acting with justice.'
segments.s548-g71: غم is the subject 'care/worry' (here 'care' or 'anxiety'); پادشاهی جهانجوی is a genitive compound 'a kingship that seeks the world' (i.e., an ambitious, world‑seeking king/kingdom). راست is the predicate adjective 'is true/real' — classical Persian often omits a copula, so 'The anxiety of a world‑seeking kingship is real.'
segments.s548-g72: ز گیتی فزونی is 'from/worldly increase' or 'despite the world's increase' (ز + گیتی + فزونی). سگالد is an archaic/poetic verbal form (sense here close to 'harrying/assailing' or 'trouble caused by others'), and نه کاست is negative past 'it was not diminished' (کاست as 'was lessened'). Together the line says that despite worldly expansion/increase, his care/sorrow was not diminished.
segments.s548-g9: چهارم is the ordinal 'fourth' introducing the rule. چنان دان is an imperative/formulaic instruction 'know thus' (دان = 'know/consider'); که introduces a clause ('that'). بیم گناه is a noun phrase 'the fear of sin' with بیم (fear) governing گناه (sin).
segments.s548-g10: فزون باشد combines the adjective فزون ('greater/more abundant') with the subjunctive/jussive form باشد ('let it be' / 'should be'), giving a prescriptive comparative. از marks the comparison 'than/from', and بند و زندان شاه is a coordinated noun phrase 'the king's fetters and prison' where the possessor (شاه) follows the possessed.
segments.s548-g11: به پنجم is an adverbial ordinal phrase 'for the fifth' / 'fifthly'. سخن مردم زشت‌گوی is a noun phrase 'the speech/words of slanderous people' (زشت‌گوی is a compound adjective 'ugly‑speaking' = slanderer) that functions as the object of the following verb.
segments.s548-g12: نگیرد here expresses a prohibitive jussive/negative sense 'do not take/bring/accept' (implied subject 'you' or general imperative). به نزد marks direction 'to/in the presence of', کسان is 'persons/people', and آب‌روی postmodifies کسان as 'people of reputation' (i.e. respectable persons). Together: 'do not bring [those words] before respectable people.'
segments.s548-g13: بگویم is a first‑person verbal formula 'I will say / let me say' (here introducing the speaker's remark). یکی تازه اندرز is a noun phrase 'one fresh piece of counsel' (یکی = 'a certain/one', تازه = 'new/fresh', اندرز = 'advice'), and نیز = 'also/too' links this remark to the previous list.
segments.s548-g14: کجا برتر از is a rhetorical question/phrase 'what/where is better than...' with برتر 'better/superior' and از marking the comparative. دیده و جان و چیز is a coordinated list 'eye (sight), life (soul), and thing (possessions)' serving as the items being compared against.
segments.s548-g15: خنک is an evaluative adjective 'pleasant/blessed'. آنک is the relative pronoun 'he who/that which', and آباد دارد جهان is a verbless/causative construction meaning 'has the world prosperous' (دارد 'has/possesses' with جهان 'the world' as the thing possessed or described as prosperous). Together: 'pleasant is he who has a prosperous world.'
segments.s548-g16: بود is the copula 'was/is'. آشکارای (from آشکارا) here functions adjectivally/adverbially 'being manifest/evident', او 'he', چون 'as/like', and نهان 'hidden'. The clause states a comparison/equivalence: 'his being manifest is as (if) hidden' → i.e. whether he is visible or concealed it is the same.
segments.s548-g57: This nominal phrase lists an item: دگر 'another' (prefacing a new item) + داد (here a noun 'justice/tribute' or more generally 'giving') with the infinitive/verb دادن 'to give' forming a verbal noun phrase; تن خویش را is the definite object 'one's own body' (تن = 'body', خویش = 'one's own', را marks the object). Word order is typical Persian: determiner/marker + verb-noun + object. The whole functions as an infinitival idea 'to give (up) one's body.'
segments.s548-g58: نگه‌داشتن is the infinitive 'to keep/guard'; دامن خویش را is its definite object 'one's own hem/skirt' (دامن literally 'hem/skirt', here idiomatic for modesty/honor). The infinitive + object construction expresses another parallel action to the previous segment.
segments.s548-g59: به فرمان یزدان is a prepositional phrase 'by/under the command of God' (به ... فرمان = 'by the command'), followed by دل آراستن, an infinitive construction 'to adorn the heart' (دل = 'heart', آراستن = 'to adorn'). Together they state an action carried out under divine command. The preposition به is used here in the sense 'by/under.'
segments.s548-g60: مرا چون تن خویشتن خواستن is another infinitival object construction: مرا ('me' as direct object, here written as م + را), چون 'as/like', تن خویشتن 'one's own body' (an appositive or comparison), and خواستن 'to want/desire'. Grammatically, 'خواستن' takes مرا as its object and the phrase چون تن خویشتن is a comparative complement: 'to desire me as (you do) your own body.' Note: the form م is a clitic of مرا in the manuscript split.
segments.s548-g61: سه دیگر here functions as an ordinal-like heading 'the third (one)'; که introduces a relative/temporal clause 'which/that', and پیدا کنی is a verb phrase with پیدا 'found/discovered' and کنی (subjunctive/present of کردن) 'you do/you find' — together 'that you find' or 'when you find it'. راستی follows as the quality under discussion 'uprightness/truth' (what pertains when you find it).
segments.s548-g62: بدور افگنی is a verbal phrase 'you will cast (it) far away / throw away' with بدور 'away, apart' marking separation and افگنی (classical form of افکنی) the 2nd-person singular present/subjunctive/imperative sense 'you cast/throw'; کژی و کاستی are the direct objects 'crookedness and deficiency' (two abstract nouns joined by و). The verb is hortative/imperative in tone: 'cast away X.'
segments.s548-g63: چهارم 'fourth' introduces the next item. که is the relative/complementizer 'that/which', از رای شاه جهان is a prepositional phrase 'from the counsel/opinion of the king of the world' (از = 'from/of', رای here = 'counsel/plan', شاه جهان = 'king of the world'). The phrase sets the source (the king's counsel) from which one must not deviate.
segments.s548-g64: نپیچی is a negative 2nd-person singular form 'do not turn/veer' (ن- prefix + پیچی), followed by دلت 'your heart' (دل + ت second-person suffix) as the subject/affected part. آشکار و نهان 'openly and secretly' qualifies the verb: 'do not let your heart turn away, either openly or secretly.' The structure is a prohibitive instruction aimed at the addressee's inner disposition.
segments.s548-g17: دگر here functions like 'as for' / 'other' (setting a new item); آنک is a relative pronoun 'who/which' introducing a relative clause; دارند is a present-tense plural verb 'have/possess'; آواز نرم is the direct object ('a gentle/soft voice') with adjective (نرم) following the noun (آواز) in typical Persian order.
segments.s548-g18: خرد دارد is a simple predicate 'he/they have wisdom' (دارد/دارند used as present 'possess'); the following و links additional possessed qualities: شرم ('modesty') and گفتار گرم ('speech' qualified by the adjective 'گرم' = fervent/earnest). The verb is understood to govern the whole coordinated list.
segments.s548-g19: به پیش کسان is a prepositional phrase 'before/in the presence of people' (به + پیش + کسان). از بهر لاف is an adverbial 'for the sake of boast(ing)' (از بهر = 'because of/for the sake of' + لاف 'boast'), and سیم (omitted from the word-list) is the direct object implied here ('silver' or 'showy display').
segments.s548-g20: به بیهوده is an adverbial phrase 'in vain'; بپراگند is a present/habitual verb 'they scatter/spread' (classical form with ب- prefix indicating present/subjunctive/habitual); بر گزاف qualifies the action 'groundlessly/excessively' (بر + گزاف). The verb relates back to the object (sīm) introduced earlier.
segments.s548-g21: ز مردم دارد ندارد کسی زان سپاس is arranged so that ز مردم ('from/among the people') sets the group; ندارد (with کسی) yields the idiom 'there is no one' (کسی ... ندارد = 'no one'); زان سپاس = 'that praise' (زان = 'from that/that'). The negation is carried by ندارد together with کسی to mean 'no person'.
segments.s548-g22: نبپسندد is a present negative verb 'does not approve/accept'; the subject آن مرد یزدان‌شناس ('that man, the God‑knower' i.e. a pious or God‑knowing person) follows the verb in this poetic inversion. Together it states that the pious man does not approve of that praise.
segments.s548-g23: میانه گزینی is a verbal-noun/phrase 'choosing the middle/moderation' (میانه = moderation/mean, گزینی = 'choosing'); بمانی is second-person singular present/subjunctive 'you remain' or conditional 'if you remain' (an implicit 'if' is understood); به جای is an adverbial/locative 'in place' / 'in your due place'. The clause is conditional/advisory in tone.
segments.s548-g24: خردمند خوانند uses خوانند (third-person plural impersonal) meaning 'they call (you)' — here 'they will call you', with خردمند 'wise' as the complement; و links the second epithet پاکیزه‌رای 'pure‑minded' which completes the predicate. The verb is impersonal/plural 'one says / people call'.

sentences.s548-s25: You all remember our words: that to take up this task is our concern.
sentences.s548-s26: Whoever, with justice and a clear heart, delights (or loves), do not sever one another's union.
sentences.s548-s27: Keep your heart at ease about four things, from which there will be goodness and benefit as well.
sentences.s548-s28: First: fear, reverence (awe) and modesty toward God — these will be your helper and guide.
sentences.s548-s1: Attend to the words of this renowned Ardashir, all of you, young and old.
sentences.s548-s2: Whoever knows that the Creator exists should be nothing but pure and God‑worshipping.
sentences.s548-s3: Know another thing: he who acquires knowledge is despised, whether he is a subordinate or a king.
sentences.s548-s4: Know three more things: that speech will never be turned against a wise, old man.
sentences.s548-s37: If he learns from his ministers and from his army that his realm is suffering.
sentences.s548-s38: If the sovereign does not seek justice for him, he is not a true ruler and is unfit for the royal crown.
sentences.s548-s39: He blackened (sullied) the imperial charter; from that moment on no honour remained for him.
sentences.s548-s40: Consider him so: as if the tyrannical lord were a fierce lion in the meadow.
sentences.s548-s13: Beyond this lie five counsels; where will your religion and ritual be made new for you?
sentences.s548-s14: It will add bodily ease and joy to you, for with his honey poison will not harm you.
sentences.s548-s15: One (counsel) is that by the bounty of a just lord you will not find a way by greed or by toil.
sentences.s548-s16: Whoever is content becomes wealthy; the flower of his new spring grows prosperous.
sentences.s548-s21: All of you, lend your ears to my counsel; my saying of useful words is beneficial.
sentences.s548-s22: It was cherished in every heart that they might obtain from him safety from harm.
sentences.s548-s23: Rest for a while from learning, if you wish to kindle (or enliven) the life (of the soul).
sentences.s548-s24: When a child is under instruction/upbringing, guard him against the world's frivolities: keep him away from play and hold him close/strict.
sentences.s548-s41: That same underling who pays no heed to the king's command with toil or effort.
sentences.s548-s42: May his life be spent in pain and suffering; may he not grow old in the sponge‑house.
sentences.s548-s43: If he finds a superior/elder, and (if) he does not find one better than Zefti and Kandauri.
sentences.s548-s44: May the hearts of our subordinates be glad; and may our justice make the world prosperous.
sentences.s548-s17: When/after you have broken the neck of greed, do not tell the secret in the presence of women.
sentences.s548-s18: Third: do not pride yourself in shame and battle; for shame and warfare bring pain and suffering.
sentences.s548-s19: Fourth: keep your heart far from sorrow; do not let your heart be sullen about what has not come.
sentences.s548-s20: Do not meddle in a matter that is not your business; do not rush upon that which is not your quarry.
sentences.s548-s33: If you love him as you love your own body, at his command the realm will be renewed.
sentences.s548-s34: Keep your heart bound to his covenant; do not let your spirit turn away from his command.
sentences.s548-s35: So love him as you love your own life; when you see your guardian acting justly.
sentences.s548-s36: The care of a world‑seeking kingship is real; worldly expansion did not lessen it.
sentences.s548-s5: Fourth: know this — let the fear of sin be greater than the king's fetters and prison.
sentences.s548-s6: Fifth: do not bring the words of slanderous people before persons of good repute.
sentences.s548-s7: I will tell one fresh piece of counsel as well: what is better than sight, life, and possessions?
sentences.s548-s8: Blessed (pleasant) is he for whom the world is prosperous; whether he is manifest or hidden makes no difference.
sentences.s548-s29: Another (thing) is to give up one's body and to guard one's own hem.
sentences.s548-s30: To adorn the heart by the command of God; to desire me as (one desires) one's own body.
sentences.s548-s31: Third: when you discover it, cast away crookedness and deficiency.
sentences.s548-s32: Fourth: do not let your heart turn away from the counsel of the king of the world, neither openly nor secretly.
sentences.s548-s9: As for those who have a gentle voice, they possess wisdom, modesty, and earnest speech.
sentences.s548-s10: In the presence of people they display silver for the sake of boasting; they scatter it in vain and extravagantly.
sentences.s548-s11: There is no one among the people who lacks that praise; the God‑knowing man does not approve of it.
sentences.s548-s12: If you keep to the middle course and remain in your place, they will call you wise and pure‑minded.
